title: Silence Isn’t Absence: Finding God in the Quiet
hook: God feels silent? Silence isn’t absence—here’s why.
script: God feels silent? Silence isn’t absence—here’s why. David cried, How long, Lord? in Psalm 13. Jesus echoed Psalm 22 on the cross. Silence is a season, not a verdict. In the quiet, God matures trust, steadies breath, and deepens roots. Try this today: sit still for two minutes, breathe the name of Jesus, read Psalm 13 aloud, and ask, Lord, what does faithfulness look like right now? Then take one small obedient step and text a friend to pray. Silence is a classroom, not a courtroom. Take heart. Wait for the Lord; be strong. Psalm 27:14.

title: 4 Biblical Reasons God Seems Silent
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. In Scripture, silence can mean formation—God growing patience (James 1:4). It can mean timing—the vision awaits its appointed hour (Habakkuk 2:3). It can mean direction—trust and He will make your paths straight (Proverbs 3:5-6). Or a gentle heart-check—if sin numbs, confession reopens the line (1 John 1:9). Your move today: whisper this prayer, Search me, God. I’m listening. Then do the last clear thing He asked and leave the results to Him. Save this for the next quiet night. You are not unseen.

title: Silence Survival Plan: Peace While You Wait
hook: If you’re tired of waiting on God, listen to this.
script: If you’re tired of waiting on God, listen to this. Here’s a simple Silence Survival Plan. Daily stillness: two unhurried minutes of quiet—In returning and rest you are saved (Isaiah 30:15). Anchor a verse on your lock screen: Wait for the Lord (Psalm 27:14). Practice simple obedience to the last instruction. Check in with one believer each week for prayer. Record three gratitudes nightly. If conscience tugs, confess and receive mercy (1 John 1:9). Do this for seven days. Notice how peace returns before answers arrive. God’s silence is not distance; it’s direction. Keep going—He’s closer than you feel.
